negative_cases.why_wrong: Indirect Branch Ddt Balance: keeps the indirect ddt equation form but forces zero derivative.
Indirect Branch Ddt Balance: integrates V(in) with a positive bias term, producing a plausible but offset trajectory.
Indirect Branch Ddt Balance: feeds back V(out) in the derivative equation, turning the required integral into a leaky response.
Indirect Branch Ddt Balance: integrates only half of V(in), preserving shape but failing calibrated samples.
Indirect Branch Ddt Balance: subtracts a bias from the derivative before integration, producing a plausible but wrong trajectory.
